title: AInvil compile gate blocks Play Mode on compile error
operationalUse: Prove that AInvil does not attempt Play Mode validation when Unity C# compilation is broken.
userGoal: Create an intentional temporary C# compile error, verify CompileBlocked evidence, skip downstream runtime validation, clean up the error, and verify compile returns to Passed.

expectedDocuments.expected: AInvil validation safety requires compile errors to block runtime validation before Play Mode entry.
Compile gate preflight checks Unity Bridge compile state and local C# build state before any Play Mode validation.
Safety regression evidence is linked as an operational validation safety artifact.

validationChecks.expected: Temporary compile error file is created only during the safety regression.
Compile gate returns CompileBlocked and reports the intentional compiler error.
Play Mode validation is not attempted while compile gate is blocked.
Temporary compile error file and csproj include are removed, then compile gate returns Passed.
Compile gate safety regression evidence is exported and productization/release reports can read it.

passCriteria: Intentional compile error file is created.
Compile gate detects the compiler error.
Temporary compile error file is deleted.
Compile gate returns Passed after cleanup.
